findings.title: Monolithic entry point (main.tsx) at 4,694 lines
findings.description: main.tsx serves as CLI parser, startup orchestrator, configuration resolver, authentication flow, feature gate evaluator, and session launcher all in one file. It imports from 60+ modules and uses lazy require() hacks to break circular dependencies. This single file is the chokepoint for every startup path.
findings.impact: Impossible to test startup paths in isolation. Any change to startup order risks regressions across all entry modes. Import-time side effects make module loading order-dependent.
findings.remediation: Decompose into discrete startup phases: (1) CLI parsing, (2) configuration resolution, (3) authentication, (4) feature gating, (5) session launch. Each phase should be an independent module with its own tests.

findings.title: God Object: bootstrap/state.ts with 60+ exported functions
findings.description: bootstrap/state.ts (1,105 lines) holds session IDs, CWD tracking, cost/token accounting, telemetry providers, hook registrations, and stats stores as module-level mutable variables. This creates massive coupling where nearly every module depends on it.
findings.impact: Any module importing bootstrap/state joins a strongly connected component, making circular dependencies inevitable. State mutation is untracked. Testing requires resetStateForTests() resetting 20+ variables.
findings.remediation: Continue the ARCH-002 extraction: split into CostTracker, SessionIdentity, TelemetryProviders, HookRegistry, and InteractionTimer modules. Each should own its state and expose a narrow interface.

findings.title: Pervasive circular dependency workarounds (45 require() calls across 20 files)
findings.description: The codebase uses dynamic require() as circuit breaker for circular imports across 45 locations in 20 files. Key cycles include main.tsx ↔ AppState.tsx ↔ teammate.ts, auth.ts in large SCC with settings, and compact modules.
findings.location: src/main.tsx:68-81, src/state/AppStateStore.ts:406, src/services/compact/microCompact.ts:34, and 17 other files
findings.impact: Dynamic require() defeats tree-shaking and bundle optimization. Module initialization order becomes fragile. Dependency analysis tools cannot understand the graph.
findings.remediation: Map the full SCC using madge tool. Extract shared types into leaf modules. Use dependency injection or event-based decoupling for auth ↔ settings and compact ↔ promptCache cycles.

findings.title: Undifferentiated utils directory: 588 files, 348 top-level exports
findings.description: src/utils/ mixes bash parsing, authentication, permissions, plugins, settings, shell operations, and model management in one flat namespace. No meaningful encapsulation boundary exists.
findings.impact: No clear alternative to utils/ when adding new code, accelerating its growth. Import paths blur subsystem boundaries. High-coupling surface prevents splitting.
findings.remediation: Promote major subsystems to top-level: src/bash/, src/auth/, src/permissions/, src/plugins/, src/settings/. Keep utils/ for true leaf utilities (array, format, string).

findings.title: Near-zero test coverage: 9 test files for 2,018 source files (0.45%)
findings.description: Only 9 test files exist. Zero tests cover query engine, tool execution, state management, pipeline, MCP client, API client (3,422 lines), permission system (2,640 lines), or any React components.
findings.impact: Refactoring is unsafe. Security-critical code (permissions, bash security) has zero automated verification. The vitest config is properly set up but unused.
findings.remediation: Prioritize tests by risk: (1) bashPermissions.ts / bashSecurity.ts -- security-critical, (2) bootstrap/state.ts -- most-coupled, (3) QueryEngine.ts -- core business logic. Target 40% coverage on critical paths before refactoring.

findings.title: Security-critical code duplication: 5,706 lines across 3 files
findings.description: Three implementations of read-only validation exist: BashTool/readOnlyValidation.ts (1,990 lines), PowerShellTool/readOnlyValidation.ts (1,823 lines), utils/shell/readOnlyCommandValidation.ts (1,893 lines). Plus bashPermissions.ts (2,640) and bashSecurity.ts (2,639) duplication.
findings.impact: Security policy changes must be applied to 3 files simultaneously or validation becomes inconsistent. A bypass fixed in one file but not others creates exploitable gaps.
findings.remediation: Extract ShellValidationEngine with shell-specific strategy adapters (BashStrategy, PowerShellStrategy). Core validation logic in one place, shell-specific syntax via adapters.

findings.title: 568 orphaned sed backup files (*-e) polluting source tree
findings.description: 568 files with -e suffix exist (artifacts from macOS sed -i operations). They appear in git status and confuse code search tools.
findings.impact: Pollutes git status. Confuses code search. Inflates file counts. May cause import resolution ambiguity.
findings.remediation: Delete: find src -name '*-e' -delete. Add '*-e' to .gitignore. Fix sed invocation pattern in scripts.

findings.title: 12 files exceeding 3,000 lines, totaling ~45,000 lines in hotspots
findings.description: Files exceeding 3,000 lines: cli/print.ts (5,596), utils/sessionStorage.ts (5,089), utils/hooks.ts (5,022), screens/REPL.tsx (4,996), main.tsx (4,694), utils/bash/bashParser.ts (4,436), utils/attachments.ts (3,997), and 5 others.
findings.impact: Large files have higher defect density, longer review times, and more merge conflicts. Functions within become tightly coupled to file-level state.
findings.remediation: Prioritize splitting high-churn files. Start with utils/hooks.ts (5,022 lines) and cli/print.ts (5,596) which likely contain many independent implementations.

findings.title: Dual state management with unclear boundaries
findings.description: Three co-existing state systems: (1) bootstrap/state.ts mutable singleton, (2) state/sessionConfig.ts extracted model/config, (3) AppStateStore.ts React-compatible immutable store. cost-tracker.ts re-exports 20+ symbols masking real dependency.
findings.impact: Unclear which state system to use for given data. bootstrap singleton not observable, so UI components must poll. cost-tracker proxy creates transitive coupling.
findings.remediation: Migrate bootstrap/state.ts fields into store.ts pattern. This enables reactive updates and eliminates cost-tracker proxy. sessionConfig.ts extraction provides template.

findings.title: Import-time side effects in entry point create fragile initialization
findings.description: main.tsx lines 9-20 execute side effects during import: profileCheckpoint(), startMdmRawRead(), startKeychainPrefetch(). These launch parallel I/O but create implicit ordering constraints.
findings.impact: Moving imports would break profiling. Bundler optimizations could silently change behavior. eslint-disable comments acknowledge violation of project's own rule.
findings.remediation: Extract initialization into explicit bootstrap() function. Import modules but defer execution. Preserves parallel I/O optimization while making ordering explicit and testable.

findings.title: Well-designed pipeline pattern underutilized
findings.description: src/pipeline/ implements proper CommandBus with middleware, context, and routing. However, main query loop (query.ts 1,729 lines) and QueryEngine.ts (1,295 lines) operate independently, suggesting incomplete adoption.
findings.impact: Two execution models coexist. New features must choose which, existing features don't benefit from pipeline middleware (logging, metrics, error handling).
findings.remediation: Audit which flows use pipeline vs direct calls. Create migration plan to route query.ts through pipeline. Pipeline middleware can subsume scattered cross-cutting concerns.

findings.title: Moderate type safety: 31 'as any' casts, 13 ts-ignore suppressions
findings.description: 31 'as any' casts across 21 files and 13 @ts-ignore/@ts-expect-error suppressions across 4 files (concentrated in src/ink/ rendering). For 522K-line codebase with strict mode, relatively low but ink/ concentration suggests gaps.
findings.location: src/ink/render-to-screen.ts (5), src/ink/ink.tsx (6), and scattered across 21 other files
findings.impact: ink/ suppressions in rendering code could cause silent visual bugs. API adapter casts could mask contract violations.
findings.remediation: Address ink/ by improving type definitions for custom React reconciler. Address API adapters with proper discriminated union types for provider responses.

recommendations.title: Delete 568 orphaned -e files and add to .gitignore
Write tests for security-critical paths
recommendations.impact: Required before any refactoring of security code

recommendations.title: Complete ARCH-002 state extraction
recommendations.impact: Continue decomposing bootstrap/state.ts following sessionConfig.ts template

recommendations.title: Map and resolve circular dependency graph
recommendations.impact: Prerequisite for all other structural changes

recommendations.title: Decompose main.tsx into startup phases
recommendations.impact: Enables isolated testing of startup paths

recommendations.title: Promote utils/ subsystems to top-level modules
